rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical methoxylation/dimerization of styrenes with methanol via anodic oxidation proceeding through a radical-cation/radical intermediate
electrochemical_role: anodic oxidation of styrene to a styryl radical cation (onset ~+1.713 V vs Ag/AgCl), which is intercepted by methanol to give a benzylic carbon radical that couples to deliver the 1,4-dimethoxy product
electron_flow: The anode performs single-electron oxidation of styrene (Eonset ≈ +1.713 V) to give a styryl radical cation; methanol adds to the radical cation generating a β-methoxy benzylic carbon radical, which is intercepted (radical coupling/further oxidation) en route to the dimethoxylated coupling product.
rate_determining_step: anodic SET oxidation of the styrene to its radical cation

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Product 2 was not detected (N.D.); BHT-trapped adduct V containing the β-methoxy benzylic fragment was detected by HRMS, supporting a carbon-radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: styrene (1)
probe_reagents.observation: CV showed an oxidation onset at +1.713 V vs Ag/AgCl, consistent with direct anodic oxidation of styrene

notes: Mechanism inferred from CV (Figure S3) and BHT trapping (Scheme S1) shown in the SI. The BHT adduct corresponds to the β-methoxy benzylic radical, indicating MeOH addition occurs before the radical-coupling step; no KIE, DFT, or EPR evidence is presented on these pages.
